prompt:
Traditional Japanese sumi-e ink wash painting (intended use).
Scene: aged rice paper surface with visible texture and subtle ink bleed, abundant negative space.
Subject: a solitary heron standing in shallow water amid a few delicate reeds, looking down.
Key details: bold confident brush strokes for the heron's body in deep black ink, soft gray washes for water reflection and distant mist, ink gradation from rich dark to pale wash; a single small red hanko seal in the lower-right corner; minimalist classical East Asian composition.
Constraints: no modern elements, no English text, no watermark, only the one hanko seal.
